# COVAX

# COVAX

Gavi co-led COVAX, the vaccines pillar of the Access to COVID-19 Tools Accelerator (ACT-A).

![OWP logo](/sites/default/files/covid/covax/pages/OWP_LOGO_covax-pages_short.png)

![OWP logo](/sites/default/files/covid/covax/pages/OWP_LOGO_covax-pages_short.png)

![](/sites/default/files/covid/covax-partners.jpg)

![](/sites/default/files/covid/covax-partners.jpg)

### About

From 2020 through 2023, COVAX, a global initiative focused on equitable access to COVID-19 vaccines, enabled the largest and fastest public health roll-out in history in the face of an unprecedented global emergency. It helped avert millions of deaths and generated invaluable learnings that underscore how the world must be better prepared for future pandemics.

Click below for COVAX’s impact, history, key learnings and participant perspectives.

[About COVAX](/covax/about "About COVAX")

Now that COVID-19 vaccines have reached billions of people worldwide, the evidence is overwhelming that no matter which one you take, the vaccines offer life-saving protection against a disease that has killed millions.

enriched_points.expanded_content: COVAX, co-led by Gavi, WHO, and UNICEF, aimed to ensure fair distribution of COVID-19 vaccines globally, targeting 2 billion doses for low-income nations.
COVAX facilitated vaccination campaigns in low-income countries, ensuring essential access to vaccines, which helped avert millions of deaths during the pandemic.
COVAX involved collaboration among multiple stakeholders, including governments and NGOs, to enhance vaccine distribution and address global health inequities.
Global vaccination efforts have significantly reduced COVID-19 infections and deaths, providing invaluable insights for future pandemic preparedness and immunization strategies.
Future initiatives will focus on strengthening vaccine supply chains, expanding immunization programs, and addressing vaccine hesitancy to enhance global health security.
